questions.text: For a single step reaction A ->B + 2C, the rate law is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because rate laws or rate equations are mathematical expressions that describe the relationship between the rate of a chemical reaction and the concentration of its reactants.
This option is incorrect because B is on the product side and we do not incorporate products while stating the rate law.
This is the correct option as here we have taken only the reactant i.e. A to form the rate law equation.
This option is incorrect because while stating the rate law equation, only reactants are considered, whereas here B is present on the products side.

questions.explanation: Rate laws or rate equations are mathematical expressions that describe the relationship between the rate of a chemical reaction and the concentration of its reactants. Rate = k[A]x[B]y Where A and B are the reactants raised to power x and y which are their stoichiometric coefficients in the balanced equation and k is the proportionality constant of the reaction.In this particular reaction, only A is on the reactant side and has no power so the rate equation becomes. rate=k[A]. The rest of the options also include products in the rate equation (options B and D) and option A does not include reactant A in the rate equation, hence these are incorrect.

questions.text: The influence of temperature on reaction rate is predicted by:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect because free energy change is concerned with the spontaneity of chemical reactions.
Option B is incorrect because Van der waal’s equation is the extension of the ideal gas law.

Increase in temperature increases the number of particles having energy equal to or greater than the activation energy, therefore the rate of reaction increases with increase in temperature. In the rate equation, factors such as change in temperature, surface area etc are compensated by changing rate constant. The relationship between the rate constant and temperature is given by the Arrhenius Equation:Therefore, the Arrhenius Equation establishes an indirect relationship between rate and temperature, and hence, option C is the correct answer.
Option D is incorrect because kinetic equations are concerned with motion.

questions.explanation: The effect of temperature on reaction rate is described by the Arrhenius equation.It shows that increasing temperature increases the rate constant exponentially

questions.text: The rate of a reaction in general can be increased by all the following factors except:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: When the temperature of the reaction is increased, the rate of reaction increases.
The addition of the catalyst increases the rate of the reaction.

options.text: by an increase in activation energy
options.explanation: The correct option is "By an increase in activation energy". Increasing the activation energy of a reaction will actually decrease the rate of the reaction as it will require more energy for the reactants to reach the transition state and proceed with the reaction. All other options listed can increase the rate of a reaction.
By increasing concentration of reactants, the rate of enzymatic reaction will increase.

questions.explanation: Activation energy is inversely proportional to the rate of the reaction. When the activation energy increases, the rate of the reaction decreases. The addition of the catalyst increases the rate of the reaction. When the temperature of the reaction is increased or the concentration of the reactants is increased, the rate of the reaction increases.

questions.text: Role of a catalyst in a chemical reaction is to:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: A catalyst is a substance that speeds up a chemical reaction, but is not consumed by the reaction; hence a catalyst can be recovered chemically unchanged at the end of the reaction it has been used to speed up, or catalyze.

questions.text: “The sum of all the exponents to which the molar concentration in terms in the rate equation are raised" defines:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The rate of a reaction is defined as the change in concentration of a reactant or a product divided by the time taken for the change. So the reaction rate decreases with time. It never remains uniform during different time periods. It decreases continuously till the reaction ceases. Rate of reaction =change in concentration /time taken for the change.

The order of reaction is given by the sum of all the exponents to which the concentrations in the rate equation are raised. The order of reaction may also be defined as the number of reacting molecules, whose concentrations alter as a result of the chemical change.

Many chemical reactions can be classified into one of five basic types. Having a thorough understanding of these types of reactions will be useful for predicting the products of an unknown reaction. The five basic types of chemical reactions are combination, decomposition, single-replacement, double-replacement, and combustion. Analyzing the reactants and products of a given reaction will allow you to place it into one of these categories. Some reactions will fit into more than one category.

The substance(s) to the left of the arrow in a chemical equation are called reactants. A reactant is a substance that is present at the start of a chemical reaction. The substance(s) to the right of the arrow are called products. A product is a substance that is present at the end of a chemical reaction.

questions.explanation: The order of reaction can be defined as the power dependence of the rate on the concentration of all reactants.

questions.text: A certain chemical reaction follows thefollowing rate law: Rate =K [A] [B]2 The order of reaction is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The order of reaction is the sum of the exponents in the rate equation, not just one of them.
This option is incorrect. The order of reaction is not simply the exponent of one reactant; it is the total of all exponents.
This option is correct. The order of reaction is the sum of the exponents in the rate equation: 1 (for [A]) + 2 (for [B]2) = 3.
This option is incorrect. The order of reaction is not the sum of arbitrary numbers but the sum of the exponents in the given rate law.
This option is incorrect. The order of reaction is determined by the actual exponents in the rate law, which sum to 3 in this case.

questions.explanation: The order of a reaction is determined by summing the exponents of the concentration terms in the rate law. In the given rate law, Rate = K [A] [B]2, the exponent of [A] is 1, and the exponent of [B] is 2. Thus, the overall order of the reaction is 1 + 2 = 3. Option C is correct. The other options are incorrect because they do not correctly sum the exponents in the rate equation.

questions.text: The unit of rate of reaction is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Incorrect as it doesn't involve the concept of time (sec) in the unit of rate.
Incorrect because the rate is typically expressed as concentration per unit time, so sec-1 represents 1/second, which means change in concentration per second.
Correct. The rate is usually expressed as moles per liter (dm-3) per unit time (sec-1), indicating the change in concentration per unit time.
Incorrect as it includes a second-order term (sec-2) in the unit, which doesn't represent the rate of a reaction.
Incorrect because it includes an inverse term for volume (dm3)-1, which doesn't represent the unit for rate.

questions.explanation: Reaction rates are usually expressed as the concentration of reactant consumed or the concentration of product formed per unit time. The units are thus moles per decimeter cube per unit time [Mole (dm-3) sec-1 ].

questions.text: The rate of a chemical reaction is directly proportional to the product of active masses of the reactants, it is referred to as:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The rate of a chemical reaction being directly proportional to the product of the active masses of the reactants is described by the law of mass action. Therefore this option is not correct.

The law of mass action states the rate of chemical reaction is directly proportional to the product of the reactant concentration values. This law gives a formula for the equilibrium constant:Kc={C}^c {D}^d/{A}^a {B}^b
The rate of a chemical reaction being directly proportional to the product of the active masses of the reactants is described by the law of mass action. Therefore this option is not correct.
The rate of a chemical reaction being directly proportional to the product of the active masses of the reactants is described by the law of mass action. Therefore this option is correct.

questions.explanation: The rate of a chemical reaction being directly proportional to the product of the active masses of the reactants is described by the law of mass action. Therefore this option is correct.

questions.text: A catalyst:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Increases the rate of the forward reaction only
options.explanation: A catalyst affects both forward and reverse reactions equally, so it does not solely increase the rate of the forward reaction. This option is incorrect.

options.text: Increases the rate of both forward and reverse reactions
options.explanation: Correct. A catalyst provides an alternative pathway with lower activation energy, increasing the rate of both forward and reverse reactions equally, allowing the system to reach equilibrium faster.
A catalyst does not alter the equilibrium position; it only helps the system reach equilibrium more quickly. Hence, this option is incorrect.

options.text: Increases the rate of the reverse reaction only
options.explanation: A catalyst affects both forward and reverse reactions equally, so it does not solely increase the rate of the reverse reaction. This option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: A catalyst increases the rate of both forward and reverse reaction, making the system reach equilibrium faster.

The unit of the rate constant is the same as that of the rate of reaction in a zero-order reaction. Therefore this option is not correct.

questions.explanation: For a zero-order reaction, the rate equation is:Rate = K [A]0Where K is the rate constant and A is the reactantSince [A]0 is equal to 1.Rate = K and hence both will have the same units.

questions.text: The rate of reaction:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The rate of reaction does not increase as the reaction proceeds. This is because the concentration of reactants decreases progressively.

The reaction rate, in chemistry, is the speed at which a chemical reaction proceeds. It is often expressed in terms of either the concentration (amount per unit volume) of a product that is formed in a unit of time or the concentration of a reactant that is consumed in a unit of time. The rate of reaction decreases as the reaction proceeds because the concentration of reactants is high at the start and with time, this concentration decreases and the reaction gets slower. So, the rate of reaction is dependent on concentration and hence, decreases with the passage of time.

options.text: Remains the same as the reaction proceeds
options.explanation: The rate of reaction does not remain the same. With the passage of time the concentration of reactants decreases as they are converted to products. While the concentration of product increases as reactants are converted to the products. Hence, in no way the rate of reaction is constant.

options.text: May decrease or increase as the reaction proceeds
options.explanation: There is only one way and no other in these processes. It cannot be said that its may or may not. Hence, this option is also incorrect.

questions.explanation: The rate of reaction is dependent on the concentration of reactants. The rate of a reaction decreases as time progresses. As a reaction progresses, the reactants are being turned into products, decreasing the concentration of the reactants, consequently, lowering the rate of reaction.

questions.text: 75% of a first order reaction is completed in 36 minutes. What is the half life of the reaction?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Completion of 75% of reaction = 25% left From 100% conc of reactants to 25% 2 half-lives have elapsed. After 1st half life, 50% reactants will be left and after 2nd half life 25% will be left. If two half-lives take 36 minutes, then the half-life for this reaction must be 18 minutes.

Completion of 75% of reaction = 25% left From 100% conc of reactants to 25% 2 half-lives have elapsed. After 1st half life, 50% reactants will be left and after 2nd half life 25% will be left. If two half-lives take 36 minutes, then the half-life for this reaction must be 18 minutes.

Completion of 75% of reaction = 25% left From 100% conc of reactants to 25% 2 half-lives have elapsed. After 1st half life, 50% reactants will be left and after 2nd half life 25% will be left. If two half-lives take 36 minutes, then the half-life for this reaction must be 18 minutes. Substituting the value of \(t = 36\) minutes, you can find \(k\). Then, use that \(k\) value to calculate the half-life. After calculating, you will find that the closest answer choice is c. 18 minutes.

Completion of 75% of reaction = 25% left From 100% conc of reactants to 25% 2 half-lives have elapsed. After 1st half life, 50% reactants will be left and after 2nd half life 25% will be left. If two half-lives take 36 minutes, then the half-life for this reaction must be 18 minutes.

questions.explanation: Completion of 75% of reaction = 25% left From 100% conc of reactants to 25% 2 half lives have elapsed. After 1st half life 50% reactants will be left and after 2nd half life 25% will be left.If two half lives take 36 minutes, then half life for this reaction must be 18 minutesSubstituting the value of \(t = 36\) minutes, you can find \(k\). Then, use that \(k\) value to calculate the half-life. After calculating, you will find that the closest answer choice is c. 18 minutes.

questions.text: What is the order of the following reaction:2NO +O2 -> 2NO2
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: A zero-order reaction means that the rate is independent of the concentration of the reactants. This is not applicable here since the reaction involves the concentrations of NO and O2.
A first-order reaction would mean that the rate is proportional to the concentration of one reactant. However, this reaction involves more than one reactant, indicating a higher order.

A second-order reaction indicates that the rate is proportional to the square of the concentration of one reactant or the product of the concentrations of two reactants. The stoichiometry here suggests a higher order.

The reaction is third-order overall because the rate is proportional to the concentration of NO raised to the power of 2 and O2 raised to the power of 1. Thus, the sum of these powers is 3, indicating a third-order reaction.

questions.explanation: The order of a chemical reaction is determined by the sum of the powers of the concentration terms in the rate law. For the reaction 2NO + O2 → 2NO2, the rate law is typically based on experimental data, but it can be inferred that the reaction is third-order: second-order with respect to NO and first-order with respect to O2. Therefore, the overall reaction order is 3. This is why Option D is correct, while Options A, B, and C do not accurately reflect the stoichiometry and concentration dependence of the given reaction.

questions.text: In general Photochemical reactions are of order:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: A first-order reaction's rate is directly proportional to the concentration of one of the reactants. However, in photochemical reactions, the rate is not proportional to the concentration of any reactant. Instead, it depends on the intensity of light.

A third-order reaction's rate is proportional to the product of the concentrations of three reactants. Photochemical reactions typically involve light-absorbing species, and their rates are not governed by the third power of reactant concentrations.

A second-order reaction's rate is proportional to the product of the concentrations of two reactants. Photochemical reactions do not follow this relationship since their rate is not determined by the product of concentrations of two reactants.

In photochemical reactions, the rate is not affected by the concentrations of the reactants. Instead, it depends solely on the intensity of light (light intensity plays a crucial role in photochemical reactions). Hence, photochemical reactions are of zeroth order with respect to the concentration of reactants.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is (d) 0. In photochemical reactions, the rate is independent of the concentrations of the reactants (zeroth order) and instead depends on the intensity of light.

questions.text: The order of reaction for which half-life is inversely proportional to the concentration of reaction is?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: A second-order reaction is a type of chemical reaction that depends on the concentrations of one second-order reactant or two first-order reactants. This reaction proceeds at a rate proportional to the square of the concentration of one reactant, or the product of the concentrations of two reactants.
This option is incorrect.Zero Order: For a zero-order reaction, the half-life is directly proportional to the initial concentration.
A first-order reaction depends on the concentration of a single reactant. The rate of a first-order reaction is directly proportional to the concentration of that reactant.
The half-life of third-order reaction depends on the initial concentration of the reactant and the rate constant, and it tends to increase as the concentration decreases.

questions.explanation: A second-order reaction is a type of chemical reaction that depends on the concentrations of a one second-order reactant or two first-order reactants. This reaction proceeds at a rate proportional to the square of the concentration of one reactant, or the product of the concentrations of two reactants.
hints: half-life is inversely proportional to the concentration of reaction

questions.text: Rate constant is denoted by?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Correct option. The rate constant in chemical reactions is universally denoted by the symbol "k". It signifies the constant of proportionality that ties the rate of reaction to the concentrations of the reactants involved.
Incorrect option. The symbol "kv" is not a standard representation of the rate constant in the context of chemical kinetics.
Incorrect option. The symbol "kf" may be used to denote the rate constant for a forward reaction in certain contexts but is not the general symbol for the rate constant.
Incorrect option. The symbol "ks" is not a recognized notation for the rate constant in standard chemical kinetics.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is "k" as it is the standard symbol used to represent the rate constant in chemical kinetics. The rate constant is a crucial parameter that connects the rate of a chemical reaction to the concentrations of the reactants. It provides insights into the speed and dynamics of the reaction under specific conditions. The other options, such as "kv", "kf", and "ks", are not standard symbols for the rate constant, although "kf" might sometimes be used to specifically denote a forward reaction rate constant, it is not the general symbol.

questions.text: Which of the following is a factor affecting the rate of reaction?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The rate of a enzymatic reaction is often influenced by the amount of substrate present. Initially, as the substrate concentration increases, the reaction rate also increases because there are more substrate molecules available for the enzyme to bind and convert into products. This relationship is described by the Michaelis-Menten equation. However, once the enzyme’s active sites are mostly saturated with substrate molecules, further increases in substrate concentration might not significantly increase the reaction rate. At this point, the reaction reaches a maximum rate, often referred to as the maximum velocity.
A catalyst increases the rate of a chemical reaction by providing an alternative reaction pathway with a lower activation energy.

The effect of temperature on the rate of a chemical reaction is described by the Arrhenius equation. Generally, increasing the temperature leads to an increase in the reaction rate. Here's why: 1. **Activation Energy:** The Arrhenius equation shows that reaction rate (k) is exponentially dependent on the activation energy (Ea). As temperature increases, more molecules have energy equal to or greater than the activation energy, making it easier for the reaction to occur. 2. **Collision Frequency:** Higher temperatures lead to increased molecular motion and collisions among reactant molecules. This higher collision frequency also contributes to a greater chance of successful collisions with sufficient energy to overcome the activation barrier. 3. **Reaction Rate Constant:** In many reactions, the rate constant (k) is directly proportional to temperature. An increase in temperature generally results in a higher rate constant and, consequently, a faster reaction. It's important to note that while an increase in temperature often accelerates reaction rates, extremely high temperatures may lead to undesirable side reactions or decomposition of reactants.
Since rate of reaction is affected by all the above factors so this option is correct.

questions.explanation: The major factors upon which the rate of a chemical reaction depends are:Amount of substrate/Concentration,Temperature,Catalyst, etc.Hence, all of the given options are correct.

questions.text: Which enthalpy change is relevant in the following processNa(s) -> Na (g) ΔH = +107
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Option A, enthalpy of atomization, is the energy required to completely separate all the atoms in a chemical substance. The separated atoms are in a gaseous state and get separated from the corresponding substance in its standard state. Sodium is solid in its standard state, and after atomization, the atoms are in their gaseous state. Hence, this is the correct answer. Option B is the enthalpy change when a solid substance melts into a liquid. Here, the state change is from a solid to a gas, no liquid state involved.Option C is the enthalpy change when a liquid is vaporized into a gas. Here, a solid is atomized to its gaseous state so, vaporization does not apply here.Option D is the enthalpy change when one mole of a substance is formed from its constituent elements. This does not describe the given reaction.

questions.text: Which one is not related to evaporation?

questions.topic: States of Matter - Gases, Liquids and Solids

options.explanation: This term refers to a process that happens without interruption. Evaporation can be continuous, but it is not a defining characteristic of the process itself.
Evaporation is indeed an endothermic process, meaning it absorbs heat from the surroundings. This is a key feature of evaporation.
This term describes a process that releases heat. Since evaporation absorbs heat, it is not exothermic, making it the correct answer to the question.

This term means that a process can occur on its own without external influence. Evaporation can be spontaneous under certain conditions, but it is not inherently linked to the definition of evaporation.

questions.explanation: Evaporation is a continuous and spontaneous process. It requires absorption of heat from surroundings, so it is endothermic. Therefore, exothermic is not related to evaporation

questions.text: The enthalpy change of the reaction H2(g) + ½ O2 -> H2O (l) under standard conditions is:

options.text: Enthalpy change of formation of water
options.explanation: This describes the enthalpy when 1 mole of water is formed from its elements under standard conditions.
This applies to acid-base reactions, not the formation of water from hydrogen and oxygen.

options.text: Both; Enthalpy change of combustion of Hydrogen and enthalpy change of formation of water
options.explanation: The reaction involves the combustion of hydrogen to form water, fulfilling both definitions under standard conditions.

options.text: Enthalpy change of combustion of Hydrogen

questions.explanation: Since 1 mole of H2 is being combusted, it can be called enthalpy change of combustion of H2. Also, 1 mole of water is being formed from its constituent elements under standard state so it can also be called standard enthalpy change of formation of water.

questions.text: Which one of the following enthalpy changes is always exothermic?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Combustion is an oxidation reaction that produces heat, and it is therefore always exothermic. Since these reactions are exothermic so the value for the enthalpy change (AH) is always negative

The enthalpy of formation is the standard reaction enthalpy for the formation of the compound from its elements (atoms or molecules) in their most stable reference states at the chosen temperature (298.15K).It may be both exothermic & endothermic.
Enthalpy of atomization is the change in enthalpy when one mole of bonds is completely broken to obtain atoms in the gas phase. For example: atomization of methane molecule.It is always endothermic.
Enthalpies of solution may be either positive or negative - in other words, some ionic substances dissolve endothermically (for example, NaCl); others dissolve exothermically (for example NaOH).

questions.explanation: Combustion is an oxidation reaction that produces heat, and it is therefore always exothermic. Since these reactions are exothermic so the value for the enthalpy change (ΔH) is always negative

questions.subject: Chemistry

In this case:Heat is being transferred from hot surroundings to the cold interior of a refrigerator, which is against the natural direction of heat flow.This process requires work, done by the refrigerator's compressor, which uses electrical energy to move heat from a colder region to a hotter one.According to the Second Law of Thermodynamics, heat cannot flow from cold to hot spontaneously.It can only happen when external energy is supplied — so the process is non-spontaneous.

questions.text: The change in heat energy in a chemical reaction at constant temperature and pressure is called:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Enthalpy is a thermodynamic quantity that represents the total heat content of a system at constant pressure. The enthalpy change in a chemical reaction, often denoted as ΔH, is the heat absorbed or released during the reaction at constant pressure. It includes both the internal energy change and the work done on or by the system.

The heat of sublimation is the energy required or released when a substance changes directly from a solid to a gas without passing through the liquid phase. This process involves both breaking intermolecular forces in the solid and overcoming the energy barrier to convert the substance into a gas.

Internal energy (U) is the sum of the kinetic and potential energies of the particles within a system. The internal energy change in a chemical reaction is denoted as ΔU. ΔU is related to the heat (q) and work (w) done in a system through the first law of thermodynamics: ΔU = q - w. At constant volume, ΔU is equal to the heat change (q) because no work is done.

The heat of formation (ΔHf) is the enthalpy change when one mole of a substance is formed from its elements in their standard states. It is often used to calculate the enthalpy change for a reaction by subtracting the sum of the heats of formation of reactants from the sum of the heats of formation of products: ΔH = ΣΔHf(products) - ΣΔHf(reactants). The standard state refers to the most stable form of an element at a specified temperature and pressure.

questions.explanation: The change in heat energy in a chemical reaction at constant temperature and pressure is called enthalpy change. The enthalpy of sublimation, or heat of sublimation, is the heat required to change one mole of a substance from solid-state to a gaseous state at a given combination of temperature and pressure. The change in the internal energy of a system is the sum of the heat transferred and the work done. Heat of formation of a compound is the change of enthalpy during the formation of 1 mole of the substance from its constituent elements

questions.text: The enthalpy change accompanying the gain of an electron by a neutral gaseous atom to form a negative ion is called:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Option A is wrong as the ionization potential, in chemistry, is the amount of energy required to remove an electron from an isolated atom or molecule.
Option B is wrong as the electronegativity is a measure of the tendency of an atom to attract a shared pair of electrons.
When an electron is added to a neutral gaseous atom to convert it into a negative ion, the enthalpy change accompanying the process is called Electron affinity enthalpy. Hence, option C is correct.
Option D is wrong as the lattice energy is defined as the energy required to separate a mole of an ionic solid into gaseous ions.
Option E is wrong as the potential energy is energy that is stored or conserved in an object or a substance.

questions.explanation: When an electron is added to a neutral gaseous atom to convert it into a negative ion, the enthalpy change accompanying the process is called Electron affinity enthalpy. Ionization potential, in chemistry, is the amount of energy required to remove an electron from an isolated atom or molecule. Electronegativity is a measure of the tendency of an atom to attract a bonding pair of electrons. Lattice energy is defined as the energy required to separate a mole of an ionic solid into gaseous ions. Potential energy is energy that is stored – or conserved - in an object or substance.

questions.text: The heat of a reaction can be calculated by using:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Option A is wrong as Joule's law is concerned with the heat produced during the passage of current through a resistance in a specific time i.e. H=I²Rt

Option B is wrong as Ohm's law is concerned with the relation of current with voltage. It states that current is directly proportional to the potential difference provided the resistance remains constant.

Hess' law states that the change of enthalpy in a chemical reaction (i.e. the heat of reaction at constant pressure) is independent of the pathway between the initial and final states of the reaction. If a chemical change takes place by several different routes, the overall enthalpy change is the same, i.e. the sum of all of the enthalpies of different routes (provided the initial and final conditions are the same). Hess's law allows the enthalpy change (delta H) for a reaction to be calculated even when it cannot be measured directly.
Faradays law states that emf of an cell is related to the N times the rate of change of electric flux.
Boyle's law states that pressure of a gas is inversely related to volume if the temperature is kept constant.

questions.explanation: Hess' law states that the change of enthalpy in a chemical reaction (i.e. the heat of reaction at constant pressure) is independent of the pathway between the initial and final states. In other words, if a chemical change takes place by several different routes, the overall enthalpy change is the same, regardless of the route by which the chemical change occurs (provided the initial and final conditions are the same). Hess’s law allows the enthalpy change (ΔH) for a reaction to be calculated even when it cannot be measured directly.

questions.text: In an adiabatic process:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Pressure is not maintained constant in an adiabatic process.
The gas is not isothermally expanded in an adiabatic process.

The adiabatic process is defined as a process in which no heat transfer takes place. This does not mean that the temperature is constant, but rather that no heat is transferred into or out of the system.

options.text: System exchanges heat with the surroundings
options.explanation: The system does not exchange heat with the surroundings because the heat exchange is zero.

questions.explanation: During an adiabatic process, the system is completely insulated from its surroundings. Thus, it takes place without heat entering or leaving the system, i.e.,q=0. During an isobaric process, the pressure of the system is kept constant (ΔP=0). During the isochoric process, the volume of the system is kept constant. (ΔV=0). During an isothermal process. the temperature of the system is kept constant (ΔT=0 but q≠0).

questions.text: Cl + e ----> Cl-ΔH = -348kJ/molThe value -348KJ/mol in this case will be:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Ionization energy is the energy required to remove an electron from a gaseous atom. Here, an electron is being added, not removed.
Electronegativity refers to an atom's ability to attract shared electrons in a bond. This process does not involve forming a bond, but rather adding an electron to form an ion.
Electron affinity is the energy released when an electron is added to a neutral atom to form a negative ion, which is exactly what occurs with Cl in this reaction.
Entropy measures the disorder or randomness in a system. It does not directly relate to the energy change associated with adding an electron to an atom.
Gibbs free energy measures the maximum reversible work done by a thermodynamic system at constant temperature and pressure. It is not specifically about adding an electron to an atom.

questions.explanation: As it can be seen in the equation, electrons are gained by the element chlorine to form a negative ion (anion). The process in which electrons are gained is called electron affinity.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is NOT true for the first law of thermodynamics?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Total energy of the system and surroundings is conserved
options.explanation: This statement is true. The first law of thermodynamics, also known as the law of conservation of energy, states that the total energy within an isolated system, encompassing both the system and its surroundings, remains constant. Energy can change forms or be transferred, but the total amount remains conserved.

options.text: Energy can neither be created nor destroyed
options.explanation: This statement is true and is a fundamental aspect of the first law of thermodynamics. Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transformed from one form to another, maintaining the overall energy balance within the system.

options.text: The first law is synonymous with the law of conservation of energy
options.explanation: This statement is true. The first law of thermodynamics is essentially the same as the law of conservation of energy, emphasizing that energy is conserved in any physical or chemical process.

options.text: Total energy of the system is increasing
options.explanation: This statement is NOT true. According to the first law of thermodynamics, the total energy of an isolated system remains constant. While energy can be transformed or transferred, the total energy does not increase or decrease, making this statement incorrect.

questions.explanation: The first law of thermodynamics, also known as the law of conservation of energy, asserts that the total energy within an isolated system is constant. Energy may be transformed from one form to another or transferred between the system and its surroundings, but it cannot be created or destroyed. Therefore, the statement in Option D is incorrect as it contradicts this fundamental principle by suggesting that the total energy of the system is increasing.Options A, B, and C correctly reflect the principles of the first law of thermodynamics, emphasizing the conservation of energy and its equivalence to the law of conservation of energy.

questions.text: The reaction 2CO + O2 -> 2CO2 proceeds slower because activation energy is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Activation energy is not related to equilibrium. Equilibrium refers to the state where the rate of the forward reaction is equal to the rate of the reverse reaction, and it doesn't provide information about the speed of the reaction or the activation energy

Activation energy is not a constant for a particular reaction. It is a specific value for that reaction under given conditions. However, it can be affected by changes in temperature and other factors.

If the activation energy is low, it means that the reactants require relatively less energy to overcome the energy barrier and transform into products. In this case, the reaction is likely to occur more quickly because a larger fraction of the molecules will have sufficient energy to react.

The activation energy of a chemical reaction is closely related to its rate. Specifically, the higher the activation energy, the slower the chemical reaction will be. This is because molecules can only complete the reaction once they have reached the top of the activation energy barrier.

questions.explanation: The activation energy of a chemical reaction is closely related to its rate. Specifically, the higher the activation energy, the slower the chemical reaction will be. This is because molecules can only complete the reaction once they have reached the top of the activation energy barrier.

questions.text: One Calorie is equal to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: 1 kilo calorie is equal to 4.18 kilojoules (kJ).This is the conversion factor commonly used in the context of nutrition and the energy content of food. It is also known as a kilocalorie (kcal).
1 calorie is equal to 4.18 joules (J).This is the smaller calorie and is often used in scientific contexts, especially in the field of chemistry and physics.
This is not a valid conversion for a calorie. It looks like a molar quantity, but it doesn't correspond to the energy content of one calorie.
This is not the correct conversion for a calorie. The correct conversion is 4.18 kJ.

questions.explanation: 1 calorie equals 4.18 J. It is very often confused with 1 kcal, which equals to 4.18 kJ

questions.text: For a reaction given below C(s) + O2 (g) -> CO2 (g) -394 kJ / molThe enthalpy of reaction is
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the positive sign would indicate an endothermic reaction, but the reaction given is exothermic.
This option is correct. The negative sign indicates that the reaction is exothermic, and the magnitude (394 kJ/mol) represents the amount of heat released per mole of reactants.
This option is incorrect. Not only is the sign incorrect (indicating an endothermic reaction), but the magnitude is also incorrect. The reaction releases 394 kJ/mol.
This option is incorrect. While the sign correctly indicates an exothermic reaction, the magnitude is incorrect. The reaction releases 394 kJ/mol, not 197 kJ/mol.

questions.explanation: The formation of carbon dioxide from carbon and oxygen is an exothermic reaction, meaning it releases heat. The enthalpy change, denoted as ΔH, is negative for exothermic reactions. In this case, the reaction releases 394 kJ/mol of energy, so the correct enthalpy change is -394 kJ/mol. The negative sign is crucial as it indicates the direction of heat flow (from the system to the surroundings). Option B correctly reflects this with -394 kJ/mol. The other options are incorrect either due to the wrong sign or incorrect magnitude.

questions.text: Starting point of Kelvin scale is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The Kelvin scale is an absolute scale given by Lord Kelvin. The starting point for this scale is marked as zero kelvin i.e. -273.15 Degrees Celsius. Hence, this is the correct option.
-400K is not the starting temperature because this scale starts from the absolute zero point.
This is also incorrect as -210k is not the starting temperature because this scale starts from the absolute zero point.
-273.15k is 0 degree Celsius. Hence, this option is also incorrect.

questions.explanation: A change of one kelvin is the same amount of temperature change as one degree Celsius, but the Kelvin scale is “absolute” in the sense that it starts at absolute zero, or what Kelvin and other scientists called “infinite cold.” (0 K = -273.15 degrees C = -459.67 degrees F.)

questions.text: A catalyst increases the rate of reaction by:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because while raising the temperature can increase reaction rates, it is not a function of a catalyst. Catalysts specifically lower the activation energy without changing temperature.

This option is incorrect as well; while increasing reactant concentration can speed up a reaction, it does not describe the role of a catalyst, which alters the reaction pathway rather than the concentration of reactants.

options.text: Providing an alternative reaction pathway with a lower activation energy.
options.explanation: This is the correct option as it accurately describes the primary function of a catalyst. By offering a different pathway, the catalyst decreases the activation energy, allowing more molecules to participate in the reaction.

options.text: Shifting the equilibrium towards the products.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because while catalysts can speed up the rate at which equilibrium is reached, they do not change the position of the equilibrium itself.

questions.explanation: A catalyst speeds up a reaction without being consumed by providing a different mechanism or pathway for the reaction to follow. This alternative pathway has a lower activation energy, which allows a greater fraction of reactant molecules to react per unit of time.

questions.text: Work done on the system is
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Work done on system in chem is positive and in phy is negative.
Work done on system in chem is positive and in phy is negative.
Work done on system in chem is positive and in phy is negative.
Work done is phy is positive and in chem is negative.

questions.explanation: Work done on the system is positive in chemistry and negative in physics.Similarly work done by the system is negative in chemistry and positive in physics.

questions.text: If pressure and volume of a gas are variable while temperature remains constant, this belongs to
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This law describes the relationship between the volume and temperature of a gas, assuming pressure is constant. It states that the volume of a gas is directly proportional to its absolute temperature.Mathematically:

Avogadro's Law deals with the relationship between the volume of a gas and the number of moles of gas, assuming pressure and temperature are constant. It states that equal volumes of gases at the same temperature and pressure contain an equal number of molecules.Mathematically:
Avogadro's Law: V∝n (Volume and amount of gas at constant temperature and pressure).

Pascal's Law is related to fluid mechanics and doesn't specifically apply to gases. It states that a change in pressure applied to an enclosed fluid is transmitted undiminished to all portions of the fluid and to the walls of its container.

questions.explanation: Boyle's law is a gas law, stating that the pressure and volume of a gas have an inverse relationship. If volume increases, then pressure decreases and vice versa, when the temperature is held constant.

questions.text: Demonstrate the correct use of article:_ ink in my pen is red.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: This is incorrect. "The" is used instead of "A" because is used to indicate that there is a specific ink in the pen that the speaker is talking about. It implies that this particular ink is known or identifiable within the conversation.

This is incorrect. "The" is used instead of "An" because is used to indicate that there is a specific ink in the pen that the speaker is talking about. It implies that this particular ink is known or identifiable within the conversation.

"The" is correct because is used to indicate that there is a specific ink in the pen that the speaker is talking about. It implies that this particular ink is known or identifiable within the conversation.
An article "the'' is required hence, this option is not suitable.

questions.explanation: The use of "The" is correct because is used to indicate that there is a specific ink in the pen that the speaker is talking about. It implies that this particular ink is known or identifiable within the conversation.

questions.text: Choose the sentence with correct punctuation:
questions.subject: English

options.text: However, it didn't take long for others to jump on board.
options.explanation: This is the correct sentence. The comma after "However" indicates a pause and properly separates the introductory element from the main clause.

options.text: However it didn't take long for others to jump on board.
options.explanation: This sentence is incorrect because it lacks a comma after the introductory word "However" which is necessary for correct punctuation.

options.text: However, it didn't take long for others to jump on board?
options.explanation: This sentence is incorrect due to the use of a question mark, which is inappropriate in the sentence.

options.text: However. It didn't take long for others to jump on board.
options.explanation: This sentence is incorrect because a period is used instead of a comma after "However" which disrupts the flow of the sentence.

questions.explanation: The correct option is: "However, it didn't take long for others to jump on board." A comma is necessary after the introductory word "However" to create a pause and connect it properly to the main clause.

questions.text: Aslam and his friends were making too much noise. Their father got really _ at them.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: Afraid is used when someone is unwilling or reluctant to do something for fear of the consequences.
Angry is used for feeling of showing strong annoyance. So, angry is correct option.
Kind is used for showing a friendly, generous, and considerate nature.
Strong is used when you're able to withstand force, pressure, or wear.

questions.explanation: Angry is used for feeling of showing strong annoyance. Since noise is unwanted, the father would get angry by it, instead of kind, strong or afraid.

questions.text: Demonstrate correct use of subject-verb agreement:They _ a letter.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: "Wrote" is the past tense of the verb "write."We use it when we talk about something someone did already — an action that happened in the past."They wrote a letter."This means they already finished writing the letter — it happened in the past.
Written Past participle of "write", usually used with has/have/had.Example: "They have written a letter."Why it’s wrong here: On its own, "They written a letter" is grammatically incorrect.

Write Base form, used with plural subjects in present tense.Example: "They write letters every day."Why it’s wrong here: The sentence seems to be in past tense, so "write" doesn’t fit unless we change the whole context.
Writes Present tense, used with he/she/it.Example: "He writes a letter every week."Why it’s wrong here: "They" is plural, so "writes" doesn’t match the subject.

questions.explanation: "Wrote" is the past tense of the verb "write."We use it when we talk about something someone did already — an action that happened in the past.

questions.text: Demonstrate control of tenses and sentence structure:Maryam _ (see) the smoke of the fire rising all around.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: A “see” is incorrect because it is plural verb whereas Maryam is Singular noun.
B “saw” is the most correct option as it is in agreement with the noun Maryam.
C “will see” is incorrect because it refers to the future but there is no context given to whether the event is happening in the future or not.
“Seen” is incorrect because it requires have/had before it.

questions.explanation: Past tenses of verbs come after nouns, in this case, the proper noun “Maryam”.

questions.subject: English

options.explanation: “Hear” is correct because in present simple tense we use first form of verb.
B and C are incorrect as they refer to a particular time phase, whereas there is no context given in the question.
B and C are incorrect as they refer to a particular time phase, whereas there is no context given in the question.
D “Hears” is incorrect as we use it with a second person pronoun. With a 1st person we will simply use “Hear”

questions.explanation: The subject in the given sentence is the “strange noise”. When referring to sounds perceived by ears, “hear” is used.

It indicates that there is a particular photograph that fulfills the listener's search criteria or expectation.

It indicates that there is a particular photograph that fulfills the listener's search criteria or expectation.
D is incorrect as an article is required here.

questions.subject: English

options.text: Plant that blooms twice a year

Option B is incorrect because "biennial" means occurring once every two years, not twice a year.

questions.text: Demonstrate correct use of articles and prepositionsDo you have _ calculator? I need it to solve this sum.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: The indefinite article "a" is used in the sentence "Do you have a calculator?" because it refers to any calculator in general, not a specific one. Here’s why the article "a" is appropriate.
B is incorrect as “An” is only used before vowels or words that have the sound of vowels. E.g An Apple, An Hour.
C is incorrect as we use definite article with specific nouns.
D is incorrect as an article is required in the sentence.

questions.explanation: The indefinite article "a" is used in the sentence "Do you have a calculator?" because it refers to any calculator in general, not a specific one. Here’s why the article "a" is appropriate.

questions.text: Fact Checking:Fact 1: Some pens don't write Fact 2: Only blue pens write Fact 3: Some writing utensils are pens If the above three statements are facts then which of the following statement will also be a fact? I. Some writing utensils don't write II. Some writing utensils are blue Ill. Some blue writing utensils don't write
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect because it contradicts Fact 1, which states that "Some pens don't write." It suggests that all writing utensils write.

Correct option is B. Since some pens don't write, some writing utensils don't write(statement I). Since there are blue pens and since pens are writing utensils, some writing utensils are blue(statement II). There is not enough information to support statement III.
Option C is incorrect because it contradicts Fact 2, which states that "Only blue pens write." It suggests that some writing utensils may not be blue.
Option D is incorrect because it contradicts Fact 3, which states that "Some writing utensils are pens." It suggests that all blue writing utensils may not be pens.

questions.explanation: "Since some pens don't write, some writing utensils don't write(statement I)". "Since there are blue pens and since pens are writing utensils, some writing utensils are blue(statement II)".There is not enough information to support statement III.

questions.text: Answer on Passage:A four-person crew from Classic Colors is painting Mr. Zohaib's house. Ahmed is painting the front of the house. Maira is in the alley behind the house painting the back. Ayesha is painting the window frames on the north side, Hamza is on the south. If Ahmed switches places with Ayesha, and Ayesha then switches places with Hamza, where is Hamza?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: In the alley behind the house
options.explanation: a) In the alley behind the houseThis option suggests that Hamza is in the alley behind the house. However, this contradicts the information provided in the passage, as it states that Maira is in the alley behind the house painting the back.

options.text: On the north side of the house
options.explanation: b) On the north side of the houseThis option suggests that Hamza is on the north side of the house. However, this contradicts the information provided in the passage, as it states that Ayesha is painting the window frames on the north side.

c) In front of the houseThis option suggests that Hamza is in front of the house. This seems plausible, as Ahmed was originally painting the front of the house, and after switching places with Ayesha, he would be on the north side (painting the window frames). Then, Ayesha switches places with Hamza, and since Ahmed was originally painting the front of the house, Hamza would now be in front of the house.

options.text: On the south side of the house
options.explanation: d) On the south side of the houseThis option suggests that Hamza is on the south side of the house. However, this contradicts the information provided in the passage, as it states that Hamza is on the south side.

questions.explanation: After all the switches were made, hamza is in front of the house. Maira is in the alley behind the house, Ahmed is on the north side, and Ayesha is on the south.

questions.text: Making Judgements:Arrange the following words in a meaningful sequence. 1.lnfection 2.Consultation 3.Doctor 4.Treatment 5.Recovery
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: a) 1, 3, 4, 5, 2This option suggests that the correct sequence is "lnfection, Doctor, Treatment, Recovery, Consultation." It starts with infection, then moves on to the doctor, followed by treatment, recovery, and finally ends with consultation. However, this sequence doesn't seem to follow a logical order of events in the context of a medical scenario.
Infection occurs first, then one visits a doctor, and after consultation, the doctor starts the treatment which is followed by recovery.
This option is incorrect. Infection occurs first, then one visits a doctor, and after consultation, the doctor starts the treatment which is followed by recovery.
This option is incorrect.Infection occurs first, then one visits a doctor, and after consultation, the doctor starts the treatment which is followed by recovery.

questions.explanation: Infection occurs first, then one visits a doctor, and after consultation, the doctor starts the treatment which is followed by recovery.
